front: lani ne na ra ya Aru.
na ra waro we ya Mira.
u ra sela ke malo maru.

back: Do you hear the night / darkness?

front: mata ne na ra sela ke tulu.
nuko ne ta ra lora ke waro.

back: The tool is on top of the house.
The child is beside the parent.

front: temu luma nu le, na ra salo.
temu luma pa le, na ra pa miso.
temu luma po le, na ra po tanu.
na ra nu kani ke pani.
ta ra po naka ko tera para.
back: You will go to a far place.

front: ta o kani ke pani welo.

back: The food is in the container.

front: tulu i ra ko sela para.
back: This tool is for distant seeing.

front: na ra nema ke ya Aru.
ta ra sona ke naru ne waro.
back: You know the rules of speech.

front: u ra sona maku ke tulu.
back: They know how to make a tool.

front: na ra rima sona waro nela.
back: I want to know how to speak well.

front: ta ra no tanu so etu se?
na ra no kani ke pani.
no na ra kani ke pani.
na ra kani ke no pani.
na ra no naka so etu nilo.
back: I do not go because of cold.

front: na ra no naka so etu i le, u ra nilo.
back: I do not go because it is cold.

front: u ra miso so etu salo maru.
back: They rest because of much work.

front: ta ra tanu so etu rima ne na?
back: Did you come because of my wish?

front: u ra naka, sia na ra naka.
lani ne ta ra ya Mira.

back: The house for the child is near.
The water from the river is clean.

front: mene re ri ra maku ke malo, ra nela.
back: The person who builds a house is good.

front: malo re na ra sela ke ri, ra maru.
back: The house that I see is big.

front: mene re na ra lano ke pani ko ri, ra melo ne na.
back: The person to whom I give food is my friend.

front: tulu re ta ra kipa ke ri, ra kasa.
back: The tool you hold is hard.

front: malo re na ra to ri, ra nilo.
back: The house where I am is cold.

front: malo i ra maru pasu malo ena.
back: This house is bigger than that house.

front: tulu i ra nela pasu tulu ena.
back: This tool is better than that tool.

front: wai i ra welo mini pasu wai ena.
back: This water is less hot than that water.

front: mene i ra riki sama pasu mene ena.
back: This person is as fast as that one.

front: malo i ra maru pasu ora.
na ra sona ke i le, u ra pa tanu.
ta ra rima ke i le, na ra naka.
u ra sela ke i le, na ra maku ke malo.
back: They see that I build a house.

front: na ra waro ke i le, ya Aru ra lisu.
back: I say that Aru is simple.

front: ta ra sano ke i le, na ra po tanu?
back: Do you believe that I will come?
I have pain in my leg.

front: kute ne ta ra kipa ke tulu.
muto ne u ra no waro.

back: The plant is on the ground.
The animal moves by the river.
The wind moves with the light / sun.
The sea is bigger than the river.
This document is mine / related to me.
The image is on the sheet / in the book.

front: na ra no rima ke naru maru.
back: I do not want big rules.

front: mene mun ra nema ke ya Aru.
kale ne ya Aru ra waki.
mene mun ra waro we naru sama.
back: People speak with the same rules.

front: senu ra lano ke naru ko mene sini.
back: A teacher gives rules to a new person.

front: mene sini ra sawi ke se?
back: What does the new person ask?

front: mene lasa ra pane ke mene sini.
back: The older person answers the new person.

front: kale ra maku ke sipu mun.
mene mun ra sena ke waro.
na ra raku ke sipu mini.
ta ra lowa ke sipu ne na?
u ra raku ke lani ne u.
sipu i ra ama ke waro lisu.
sipu ena ra lasa pasu sipu i.
back: That text is older than this text.

front: na ra kame ke sipu i.
na ra niso ke lani se.
o raku ke mona ne ta.
sipu ra pawa wena ke mene.
tulu i ra lowa ke sipu.
tulu i ra pane ke i le, waro ra lisu.
back: This tool answers that speech is simple.

front: na ra maku ke tulu ne sela waro.
back: I make a tool for seeing speech.

front: mene ra pawa lowa ke waro we tulu.
back: A person can read speech with a tool.

front: tulu ra no sona ke lani se.
back: The tool does not know some name.

front: etu tulu ra no sona le, mene ra pane.
back: If the tool does not know, a person answers.

front: tulu ra maku ke pane riki.
back: The tool makes a fast answer.

front: naru lisu ra wena ke tulu.
temu luma nu le, kale ra puka ke sipu.
back: Today the community opens a text.

front: mene mun ra lowa ke sipu puka.
back: Many people read the open text.

front: mene se ra tanu so tera para.
back: Someone comes from a far place.

front: mene se ra lora ke waro ne ya Aru.
back: Someone hears the speech of Aru.

front: na ra lano ke pane ko ta.
back: I give the answer to you.

front: etu pane ra nela le, na ra toma ke i.
back: If the answer is good, I keep it.

front: etu pane ra keto le, na ra sena ke i.
back: If the answer is bad, I test it.

front: waro ne kale ra ruma maru.
back: The speech of the community becomes important.

front: ya Aru ra rima ke waro lisu.
mene un ra naka so malo.
back: One person goes from the house.

front: u ra kipa ke tulu rupi.

back: The friend is by the river.

front: melo ra lano ke wai ko u.
back: The friend gives water to them.

front: u an melo ra tanu ko malo.
back: They and the friend come to the house.

front: temu luma pini le, u ra miso.
back: At the end of the day, they sleep.

front: sipu i ra mini, tune sipu i ra nela.
back: This text is small, but this text is good.

front: mene ra lowa ke sipu i, sia mene ra waro.
back: A person reads this text, therefore the person speaks.
